questions.question: What does this codebase do at a high level?
questions.answer: This is a dbt analytics codebase that transforms e-commerce data into business-ready data marts for order analytics, customer insights, product management, and operational reporting. It processes raw order, product, customer, and supply chain data through staging models into semantic data marts with calculated metrics for financial reporting and business intelligence.

citations.description: Defines business logic for order items including revenue calculations and item classification for analytical reporting
Defines data mart for order analytics with key order details, totals, timestamps, and item breakdowns
Transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollars, and truncating timestamps

questions.question: What are the main data flows and where does data come from?
questions.answer: Data flows from raw sources through staging models to analytical marts. Source data includes: 1) e-commerce order data (orders, order items), 2) product catalog data, 3) customer data, and 4) supply chain cost data. The lineage shows 6 dbt sources and 6 seeds feeding into 15 staging models, which then transform into 15 mart models. Key flows: stg_orders → orders mart; stg_products + stg_supplies → order_items mart; stg_customers + stg_orders → customers mart.

citations.description: Transforms raw e-commerce order data as first step in order analytics pipeline
Joins staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product

questions.question: What are the critical modules that a new engineer should understand first?
questions.answer: Start with these 5 core modules: 1) models/marts/order_items.yml (business logic for revenue calculations), 2) macros/cents_to_dollars.sql (financial unit conversion used throughout), 3) models/marts/orders.sql (core order mart with item aggregation), 4) models/staging/stg_orders.sql (primary staging transformation), and 5) models/marts/customers.yml (customer analytics foundation). These represent the highest PageRank modules and cover the main business domains.

citations.description: Top PageRank module defining core business logic for order items and revenue calculations
Critical utility macro used for consistent financial reporting across all monetary values

questions.question: Where are the highest-risk areas and technical debt?
questions.answer: The codebase has 7 dead-code candidates (unspecified modules) representing technical debt. However, there are 0 architectural hubs, 0 circular dependencies, and 0 parse errors, indicating good structural health. The main risk areas are likely in the dead code modules that should be cleaned up. No high-risk architectural patterns were detected in the analysis.

citations.description: 7 dead-code candidates identified but specific modules not named in evidence
0 hubs and 0 circular dependencies indicate good architectural health

questions.question: What are the blind spots — areas where the analysis may be incomplete?
questions.answer: Key blind spots: 1) 7 dead-code candidates identified but not named, 2) lineage shows 2 dynamic transformations not detailed, 3) source system configurations and connection details not visible, 4) testing coverage and data quality checks not documented, 5) deployment pipelines and environment configurations not shown, 6) specific business logic for complex calculations may need deeper inspection beyond YAML definitions.

citations.description: 7 dead-code candidates identified but specific modules not named
2 dynamic transformations present but not detailed in evidence
